rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA WILLIAMS et al VS. SWISSPORT NORTH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motions (1) Plaintiffs Motion For Order Continuing The Hearing On Defendants Motion To Deny Class Certification And Strike Paga Allegations And Set Briefing Scheduled Therefor; (2) Defendants Motion For Order To Clarify The September 17 Order Granting Plaintiffs Leave To File Surreply
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2015, Line 16, DEFENDANT SWISSPORT USA, INC.'S Motions (1) Plaintiffs Motion For Order Continuing The Hearing On Defendants Motion To Deny Class Certification And Strike Paga Allegations And Set Briefing Scheduled Therefor; (2) Defendants Motion For Order To Clarify The September 17 Order Granting Plaintiffs Leave To File Surreply. Plaintiff Williams' Motion to Continue Defendant's Motion To Deny Class Certification And Strike PAGA Allegations is GRANTED. Defendant's Motion to Deny Class Certification is continued until October 21, 2015. On September 8, Plaintiff filed its Opposition to Defendant's Motion to Deny Class Certification and Strike PAGA Allegations. On September 17, Defendant filed its Reply to its Motion. Defendant Swissport North America, Inc. et al.'s Motion for Clarification is GRANTED. On September 17, 2015, Plaintiff filed an ex parte application for an Order Granting Plaintiffs Leave to File a Surreply. The court strikes the following language from its September 17th Order: "Defendant is ordered to make the nine declarants referenced in its Notice of Errata available for deposition." Should Plaintiff seek to compel depositions of additional declarants, the proper motion to file is Motion to Compel, not an ex parte Application for Leave to File a Surreply. rulings.Case Title: NISSIM NINIO VS. WILLIAM AYOUBI, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT WILLIAM AYOUBI'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Jeffrey Hayden's Motion to Withdrawal as Attorney of Record for Defendant Ayoubi is DENIED. California Rules of Professional Conduct, Rule 3-700(C) establishes that an attorney may withdraw when "conduct renders it unreasonably difficult for the member to carry out the employment effectively . . . ." Hayden, however, does not sufficiently demonstrate that he has "taken reasonable steps to avoid reasonably foreseeable prejudice to the rights of the client, including giving due notice to the client [and] allowing time for employment of other counsel . . . ." (California Rules of Professional Conduct, Rule 3-700(A).) Rather, Hayden presents this motion one month before trial. Hayden alleges that he made this motion at the earliest possible time, but does not specify at what date Defendant Ayoubi asked him to stop communicating with him. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NISSIM NINIO VS. WILLIAM AYOUBI, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff Ninios Fourth, Sixth And Twelfth Affirmative Defenses To Complaint In Intervention, And Of Intervenors First Cause Of Action As Against Plaintiff Ninio
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2015, Line 5, INTERVENOR MARCELA PACALDO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff Ninios Fourth, Sixth And Twelfth Affirmative Defenses To Complaint In Intervention, And Of Intervenors First Cause Of Action As Against Plaintiff Ninio. Intervenor Marcela Pacaldo's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Plaintiff Ninio's Fourth, Sixth And Twelfth Affirmative Defenses To Complaint In Intervention, and of Intervenors First Cause Of Action As Against Plaintiff Ninio is DENIED. "Mrs. Pacaldo asks this Court to find that, at the time of Angelina's death, Angelina owned 100% of the shares of Dnyamic Motors. The rationale for this finding is that, during her management of Dynamic Motors, she owned 100% of the shares of corporation, and that the purported transfers of those shares to Ninio are not enforceable under principles of contract and corporations law." Plaintiff Ninio wholly disagrees. Plaintiff Ninio presents a Stock Purchase and Sales Agreement, which demonstrates that Angelina Pacaldo purported to transfer her interest in Dynamic Motors to Plaintiff Ninio three months before Ms. Pacalso passed. Whether the Agreement is void for illegality as Plaintiff Ninio made Angelina Pacaldo the "figurehead" owner of Dynamic Motors to allegedly defraud creditors after Plaintif Ninio's bankruptcy, is an issue that is properly reserved for trial on October 26, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK LAMBANDRAKE VS. TIMOTHY ALAN BENSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment is denied. The undisputed fact that the wheels of Mr. Benson's car were not turned to the right along with Plaintiff's deposition testimony, raises a triable issue of fact as to whether Mr. Benson's vehicle was in fact making a left turn. (Plaintiff's Frederick Lambandrake's Deposition at 47:18 -48:10 and 51:19-22.) Thus, Plaintiff may pursue its negligence premise liability claim against Defendant on the basis that Defendant failed to post signs prohibiting left turns out of the parking garage. Defendant's summary adjudication motion, in regards to Plaintiff's premise liability causes of actions based on a willful failure to warn and a dangerous condition of public property, is granted. Premise liability claims based on California Civil Code § 846 and California Government Code § 830 are inapplicable in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOANNE MARIE ANNUZZI et al VS. HENRY FONG PAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Joanne Annuzzi And Osvaldo Martinez'S Motion To Compel Inspection Of Property
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 25, 2015, Line 1, Plaintiffs Joanne Annuzzi And Osvaldo Martinez' Motion To Compel Inspection Of Property. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and Rebecca.woodson@dentons.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BETSY W. BLISS ET AL VS. YUEN T. GIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs (1) Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Sets One And Two And Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, And (2) Production Of Responsive
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT LAW OFFICE OF YUEN T. GIN, YUEN T. GIN, INC., YUEN GIN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs (1) Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Sets One And Two And Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, And (2) Production Of Responsive. This is a discovery matter and the court will not provide a court reporter. The parties are to meet and confer prior to the hearing and agree on a reporter if they desire one. The court orders the following: Plaintiffs' failure to verify their responses to defendants' Special Interrogatories (Set One) renders the conclusions to their responses to the disputed interrogatories (" . . . not applicable") ineffective and that portion of their responses will be disregarded. Plaintiffs' objections for lack of relevance and invasion of privacy are overruled to the extent that defendants' Special Interrogatories (Sets One and Two) and Requests for Production of Documents (Sets One and Two) seek information concerning the resources of Betty Whelden. Said objections are otherwise sustained. Plaintiffs' objections for vagueness, ambiguity, over breadth, uncertainty, burdensomeness, oppression, argumentativeness, adoption of assumptions, and form are overruled. Plaintiffs shall provide a verified further response to Special Interrogatory No. 4 and verified further responses and responsive documents to Requests for Production Nos. 1, 3-4 (balances only - transaction data redacted), and 5 (credit balances only - debit balances and transaction data redacted) within 30 days after entry of an appropriate protective order. Plaintiff Bliss as attorney-in-fact for Whelden shall provide verified further responses and responsive documents to Requests for Production Nos. 3, 4 and 9 (as to financial assets only) within 30 days after entry of an appropriate protective order. The parties shall meet and confer as to an appropriate form of protective order. To the extent that plaintiffs contend that any Special Interrogatory or Request for Production is not applicable because the event or condition referred to therein did not occur or exist, plaintiffs' further responses shall state the basis of such inapplicability unequivocally and without ambiguous reference to their objections or understandings. To the extent that plaintiffs' responses are limited to time periods other than as stated in the Special Interrogatory or Request for Production, plaintiffs shall state the basis of such limitation unequivocally and without ambiguous reference to their objections or understandings. Both requests for sanctions are denied. rulings.Case Title: MONZERRAT PRESTEGUI MENDOZA VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT,A GOVERNMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2015, Line 17, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, SARA SHENKEN-RICH, RYAN YOUNG, DANIEL BRIDGES, GINI DOLD'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 4, 5, 6, and 7 because those claims fail to state a cause of action. A 42 USC 1983 cause of action cannot be premised on a violation of IDEA. See Blanchard v. Morton Sch. Dist. (9th Cir. 2007) 509 F.3d 934, 936. A claim under CC 54.1 "is 'intended to secure to disabled persons 'the same right as the general public to the full and free use' of facilities open to the public.' [citation] Its focus is upon physical access to public places." Turner v. Ass'n of American Med. Coll. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1401, 1412. Plaintiff has no such allegations. Plaintiff's claim pursuant to Gov't Code sec. 11135 fails to state a cause of action because that statute is limited to equitable relief and plaintiff does not seek such relief. In fact, if plaintiff did seek equitable relief, then defendant's exhaustion argument would have more merit. The cause of action for violation of CC 52.1 fails to state a cause of action. There is no allegation that defendants interfered with plaintiff's rights by threat, intimidation, or coercion. The court rejects the exhaustion argument. While the complaint refers to plaintiff's education plan, the lack of an aide, and insufficient counseling, the thrust of the action is to seek damages for the assault. Plaintiff's claims are not cognizable in the first instance in front of the agency. Plaintiff is not seeking "(1) monetary relief as the 'functional equivalent' of a remedy available under the IDEA, (2) 'prospective injunctive relief to alter an IEP or the educational placement of a disabled student,' or (3) 'to enforce rights that arise as a result of a denial' of a FAPE [free appropriate public education." C.O. Portland Public Schools (9th Cir. 2012) 679 F.3d 1162, 1168. Defendant's primary jurisdiction argument has merit. The agency will resolve whether the school improperly removed the aide and violated plaintiff's IEP. That determination will clearly affect how the negligence cause of action is resolved. Accordingly, the court stays this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
